summary: ICP canister project (canister ID: erwl3-piaaa-aaaau-ae5la-cai) with a bot feature. Currently experiencing a stopped canister error when calling startBot.
architectural_notes: Deployed on Internet Computer Protocol (ICP); canister ID: erwl3-piaaa-aaaau-ae5la-cai
known_issues: IC0508: Canister erwl3-piaaa-aaaau-ae5la-cai is stopped — startBot call rejected with reject code 5

existing_features.title: In the BotControlPanel and the useQueries hooks, wrap the startBot mutation call with error handling that detects IC0508 (canister stopped / reject code 5) and surfaces a clear, human-readable error message to the user instead of a raw rejection dump.

Add a canister availability pre-check in the startBot and stopBot mutation flows: before invoking the mutation, call the lightweight query method (ping/getStatus) and if it fails or indicates the canister is unavailable, abort with a friendly error rather than letting the update call fail with a raw IC error.

new_feature_requests.summary: Add a canister health/status check in the backend that can be called even when the bot is in a stopped or idle state, so the frontend can determine canister availability before attempting state-changing calls like startBot.

projectName: Handle stopped canister state gracefully and add canister health check
